reason: The statement does not apply to the text fragment as Saul, the protagonist in this passage, is not depicted as a bumbling tyrant or a tragicomic king. Instead, he is initially portrayed as a choice young man, goodly, and of noble stature, chosen by God to be the king over Israel. While his story does take tragic turns later on, at this point in the narrative, he is not characterized as a bumbling tyrant or tragicomic figure.
